scholarships.name: Merit Excellence Award
scholarships.criteria: Minimum CGPA 7.0, minimum 60 credits completed, must maintain 16 credits per semester

scholarships.name: Industry Partner Fellowship
scholarships.criteria: Must be enrolled in VLSI Design course as part of scholarship agreement

scholarships.name: Dean's Scholarship
scholarships.criteria: Minimum CGPA 7.0, must maintain at least 15 credits per semester

regulations.title: Maximum Credit Limit Per Semester
regulations.description: Students may enroll in a maximum of 21 credits per semester. Exception: graduating students in final semester may enroll up to 24 credits with approval.

regulations.title: Prerequisite Enforcement
regulations.description: Students must complete all listed prerequisites before enrolling in a course. Waiver available if student completed an equivalent course with grade B or better.

regulations.title: Add/Drop Deadline
regulations.description: Course add/drop requests must be submitted before the semester add/drop deadline. After the deadline, enrollment and drop operations are locked.

regulations.title: Fee Clearance for Enrollment
regulations.description: Students with outstanding fees exceeding ₹0 are blocked from new course enrollments and hostel allocations until fees are cleared.

regulations.title: Hostel Gender Restriction
regulations.description: Students may only be allocated to hostel blocks matching their gender designation. Male students to male blocks, female students to female blocks.

regulations.title: Scholarship Minimum Credit Maintenance
regulations.description: Students receiving scholarships must maintain a minimum credit load per semester as specified in their scholarship agreement. Dropping below triggers scholarship review.

regulations.title: Academic Probation Restrictions
regulations.description: Students on academic probation may not enroll in more than 15 credits per semester and must receive faculty advisor approval for course changes.

regulations.title: Graduation Credit Requirement
regulations.description: Students must complete a minimum of 160 credits to be eligible for graduation. Department-specific requirements may apply in addition.

regulations.title: Hostel Checkout Deadline
regulations.description: Graduating students must initiate hostel checkout by April 30th. Failure to initiate checkout blocks graduation clearance.

regulations.title: Exam Schedule Conflict Resolution
regulations.description: If a student has two exams scheduled at the same date and time, the department must reschedule one exam to a non-conflicting slot. No student may have two exams on the same day.

regulations.title: Scholarship Priority in Enrollment
regulations.description: When enrollment capacity is limited, students with scholarship dependencies on a specific course receive priority over merit-based ordering (CGPA).

regulations.title: Fee Deferral Policy
regulations.description: Fee deferral is available only to students with an active scholarship covering at least 50% of their total fees.

regulations.title: Credit Overload Exception
regulations.description: Credit overload exception is approved ONLY if student is in final semester AND short 3 or fewer credits of the graduation requirement.

regulations.title: Prerequisite Waiver Policy
regulations.description: Prerequisite waiver is approved ONLY if the student has completed an equivalent course with grade B or better.

regulations.title: Academic Probation Clearance
regulations.description: Academic probation status should be cleared when a student's CGPA recovers to 6.0 or above for two consecutive semesters.

regulations.title: Hostel Room Capacity Limit
regulations.description: Each hostel room has a maximum capacity of 2 students. No room may exceed this capacity.

regulations.title: Enrollment Seat Capacity
regulations.description: Course enrollment may not exceed the stated maximum capacity. When a course is full, no additional enrollments are accepted.

regulations.title: Minimum CGPA for Course Registration
regulations.description: Students with CGPA below 4.0 are not permitted to register for new courses until they complete remedial requirements.

regulations.title: Faculty Invigilator Availability
regulations.description: Each exam must have an assigned faculty invigilator. A faculty member may not invigilate two exams at the same date and time.

regulations.title: Exam Room Capacity
regulations.description: Each exam room has a maximum seating capacity. No exam may be assigned to a room that is already booked for the same date and time slot.

regulations.title: Course Drop Impact on CGPA
regulations.description: Dropping a course after the midterm period results in a 'W' (withdrawal) grade recorded on the transcript, which does not affect CGPA.

regulations.title: Inter-Department Enrollment
regulations.description: Students may enroll in courses from other departments as electives, subject to the same prerequisite and capacity rules.

regulations.title: Payment Recording Policy
regulations.description: All fee payments must be recorded immediately. Partial payments are accepted and reduce the outstanding balance accordingly.

regulations.title: Hostel Transfer Policy
regulations.description: Hostel transfers require fee clearance and the target room must have available capacity. Transfers between gender-restricted blocks follow gender matching rules.

regulations.title: Duplicate Enrollment Prevention
regulations.description: A student may not be enrolled in the same course more than once in the same semester. Duplicate enrollment attempts are rejected.

exceptions.eligibility_conditions: Student must be in final semester AND short ≤3 credits of graduation requirement (160 credits)
Student must have completed an equivalent course with grade B or better
Student must have an active scholarship covering ≥50% of total fees
Not available — capacity overrides are not permitted
Not available — gender restrictions are not waivable
Not available — deadline extensions are not permitted
Not available — probation enrollment limits are not waivable
Student may request a review if within 5 credits of graduation and has extenuating circumstances
Not available — room capacity limits are strict
Exam reschedule allowed only when student has a documented conflict
Not available — scholarship credit requirements are non-negotiable
Not available — minimum CGPA for registration is not waivable
Late fee payment accepted with ₹500 late fee surcharge
Probation may be cleared if CGPA has recovered to 6.0+ for current semester
Checkout deadline extension of 15 days available with department head approval

notifications.content: REGULATION UPDATE: For graduating students in their final semester, the credit limit has been temporarily raised from 21 to 24 credits. Effective immediately. Ref: REG-2024-TEMP-07
WARNING: The add/drop deadline is approaching. Course modifications will be locked after the deadline.
ALERT: Course enrollment approaching capacity. Only limited seats remain.
REMINDER: Outstanding fees must be cleared before any administrative action can proceed.
WARNING: Dropping courses may affect your scholarship eligibility. Please verify credit requirements.
NOTICE: This student is on academic probation. Special restrictions apply to enrollment changes.
NOTICE: Hostel checkout deadline is April 30, 2025. Graduating students must initiate checkout.
ALERT: Exam schedule conflict detected. Two exams are scheduled at the same time.
INFO: All administrative changes are logged in the audit trail for compliance purposes.
CHECKLIST: Graduation eligibility requires: (1) 160+ credits, (2) fee clearance, (3) no academic holds, (4) hostel checkout initiated, (5) all course requirements met.
